q: Echoes are produced where a sound wave is reflected from a large and hard surface.
An echo is a repetition of sound due to the reflection of sound.
The reflected sound can be heard separately from the original sound if the source is very far from the observer.
Reflection of sound obeys the laws of reflection.
Reflection of sound violates the laws of reflection.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, walls are covered with padding.
Floors are covered with rugs and carpets to reduce the effect of echo.
Padding, rugs, and carpets reflect the incident sound.
Padding, rugs, and carpets absorb the incident sound.
The amount of sound reflecting off wall surfaces is increased by padding.
Echo can be used to determine the distance $d$ between a source of sound and the reflecting surface.
The formula $v = \frac{2d}{t}$ is used to determine distance using echo.
In the echo formula, $v$ is the speed of sound and $t$ is the echo time.
Bats use echolocation to navigate and locate their prey.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the echoes.
A reverberation is a reflection of light or sound waves.
A reverberation is a wide reaching impact of an action.
Sound bouncing around in a large speaker is an example of reverberation.
Reverberation is the repetition of sound from a single reflection.
Padding and rugs increase the amount of sound reflecting off surfaces.
